name: VERDE RESOURCES, INC.
business: Verde Resources sells proprietary sustainable road construction materials including BioAsphalt (biochar-infused asphalt), Verde V24 (cold mix biochar asphalt emulsifying agent), and TerraZyme (enzyme-based soil stabilization). It also generates and sells carbon removal credits. The company licenses its technologies to partners like Ergon for manufacturing and distribution, targeting infrastructure projects.

exposures.sentence: We are a road construction and building materials company offering proprietary, environmentally sustainable materials and seeking to redefine our industry with a clear mission: enabling the Transition to Zero.
We also offer a licensed proprietary cold mix biochar asphalt emulsifying agent, which we call Verde V24 .
Further, we expect to generate revenues from the generation by our products, and our subsequent sale, of carbon removal credits .

As the rise of artificial intelligence accelerates data center energy consumption, which is now projected to account for a growing share of global electricity use, enterprises worldwide are facing increasing pressure to offset their carbon footprints.

catalysts.event: Issuance of world first carbon removal credit from asphalt production certified by Puro.earth
catalysts.sentence: The issuance to our company in April 2025 of the world s first carbon removal credit from asphalt production and application, certified by Puro.earth, the leading global registry for engineered carbon removal.

catalysts.event: Preliminary performance results from NCAT for BioAsphalt
catalysts.sentence: Technological validation of our BioAsphalt by the National Center for Asphalt Technology (NCAT), including receipt of preliminary performance results from NCAT in July 2025 that demonstrated consistent durability, particularly under low-volume roadway conditions.

catalysts.event: NCAT evaluation of cold recycling mix with 100% RAP exceeds specifications
catalysts.sentence: Most recently, in September 2025, NCAT s latest evaluation of our cold recycling mix using 100% reclaimed asphalt pavement ( RAP ) from laboratory testing demonstrated that our cold-recycled mix not only meets but exceeds industry specifications for cold-recycled asphalt.

catalysts.event: Execution of exclusive license agreement with Ergon Asphalt & Emulsions for North America
catalysts.sentence: On October 10, 2025, Verde Renewables, Inc. ( Verde Renewables ), our wholly owned subsidiary, entered into a license agreement with Ergon (the Ergon License ), pursuant to which we have granted Ergon an exclusive, non-transferable license to use, manufacture, commercialize, market, sell and distribute any product that contains or is manufactured or formed by Ergon using our proprietary Verde V24 cold mix biochar asphalt emulsifying agent in the United States (including its territories), Canada and Mexico.

catalysts.event: Deadline for funding $3 million to C-Twelve for loan and license fee
catalysts.sentence: We are required to fund the C-Twelve Loan and the $1 million licensing fee to C-Twelve (the proceeds of which are expected to be used by C-Twelve in part to enhance its Verde V24 manufacturing capability) within thirty (30) days of closing of a transaction in which our Common Stock becomes listed on a U.S. national exchange, provided that if such funding is not achieved by July 31, 2026, C-Twelve shall have the right, on ten (10) business days notice, to hold us in breach of the Joint Development Agreement.

tamClaims.claim: Global road construction market valued at $572.9 billion in 2023
tamClaims.sentence: The global road construction market was valued at $572.9 billion in 2023, according to Market Research Future s Road Maintenance Market Report (January 2025).

tamClaims.claim: North American road construction market reached $188.0 billion in 2024
tamClaims.sentence: In 2024, the North American market reached $188.0 billion, as reported by IBISWorld.

tamClaims.claim: Global carbon dioxide removal credit market could surge to $100 billion per year by 2030-35
tamClaims.sentence: the global market for carbon dioxide removal credits could surge from roughly $2.7 billion in 2023 to as much as $100 billion per year by 2030 35, if key obstacles are resolved, including clarity on credit standards, stronger corporate guidance, and robust policy frameworks.

tamClaims.claim: United States consumes approximately 400 million tons of asphalt annually
tamClaims.sentence: With the United States consuming approximately 400 million tons of asphalt annually according to an article entitled The Advancement of Asphalt Pavements Over the Last 50 Years by Drs. Jon Epps, Ph. D., P.E., and Dave Johnson, P.E., dated August 1, 2021, and published in Asphalt Magazine , the potential for credit generation is substantial.

tamClaims.claim: US produces over 600 million tons of biogenic waste yearly, enough for up to 100 million tons of biochar and 2 billion tons of carbon-sequestering asphalt
tamClaims.sentence: however, according to a report by the U.S. Department of Energy from March 2024 entitled 2023 Billion-Ton Report: An Assessment of U.S. Renewable Carbon Resources , the United States produces over 600 million tons of biogenic waste each year, which is enough to yield up to 100 million tons of biochar and enable the production of more than 2 billion tons of carbon-sequestering asphalt.
